ERROR.GEOLOCATION_NOT_SUPPORTED: Geolocation is not supported by your browser

LABEL.OBJECT_TYPES_HELP: Filter by object classification tags from ANTARES

MESSAGE.ABOUT_PARA_1: Sky2nite helps amateur astronomers discover what celestial objects are visible from their location using real-time data from the ANTARES astronomical alert broker.
MESSAGE.ABOUT_PARA_2: ANTARES (Arizona-NOIRLab Temporal Analysis and Response to Events System) processes real-time alerts from the Vera C. Rubin Observatory's Legacy Survey of Space and Time (LSST) and other surveys, identifying transient astronomical phenomena.
MESSAGE.ABOUT_PARA_3: Most amateur telescopes can observe objects up to magnitude 12-14 in good viewing conditions. Darker skies and larger apertures allow viewing of fainter objects.
MESSAGE.APP_DESCRIPTION: Discover astronomical objects visible tonight from your location using real-time data from the ANTARES broker and the Vera C. Rubin Observatory.
MESSAGE.LOCATION_SUBTITLE: Enter your location to see which astronomical objects are visible tonight
MESSAGE.MAGNITUDE_HELP: Brighter objects have lower magnitude values. Most consumer telescopes can see objects up to magnitude 12-14 in good conditions.
MESSAGE.NO_OBJECTS_DESCRIPTION: No astronomical objects matching your criteria are visible tonight from your location. Try adjusting the magnitude threshold or removing object type filters.
MESSAGE.SEARCHING_SUBTITLE: Querying ANTARES database and calculating visibility
